question: What are the main functions of the RCS (Reactor Coolant System)?

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components within the NuScale reactor coolant system. It specifies the types of steel and alloys used for various parts, their protective claddings, and their compliance with relevant BPVC material specifications to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain proper coolant flow, water chemistry, and boron concentration to protect fuel integrity and support safe operation under normal and transient conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0012.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Leakage Detection
nuscale_ch05_0012.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) does not use traditional components with designed leakage rates, making it impractical to distinguish between identified and unidentified leakage inside containment using current instrumentation. All leakage into containment is treated as unidentified until its source is confirmed, with leakage rates determined by RCS inventory balance and compared to containment evacuation system (CES) flow rates; the detection systems are reliable and support leak-before-break analyses.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is responsible for removing heat from the reactor core and transferring it to the steam generators using natural circulation, driven by density and elevation differences. The RCS includes key components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated valves, and interfaces with several other systems for safety and operational control, including degasification and emergency core cooling. The system is designed to maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary and prevent the release of radionuclides.

edges.description: Section A provides detailed component and material information about the pressurizer, which is one of the subsystems described in Section B's general summary of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) and its main functions.

Section A describes the physical components and materials of the RCS, while Section B discusses the detection of leakage from the RCS boundary, both addressing different aspects of the RCS's design and operational integrity.

Section A details the specific components and materials of the pressurizer, which is a subsystem of the RCS described in Section B as performing functions such as degasification and pressure retention.

Section A describes the main functions and design features of the RCS, while Section B discusses the implications of the RCS design (specifically the absence of traditional leakage paths) on leakage detection methods, thus Section B provides details about operational consequences based on the design described in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview of the RCS's main function—circulation of primary coolant—while Section B specifies additional detailed functions such as degasification and pressure boundary integrity, thereby elaborating on the general description in Section A.

Section B describes the main functions of the RCS, including maintaining the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section A discusses how leakage detection is implemented to monitor and ensure that integrity, thus connecting the operational function (B) with the monitoring method (A).
